cost_components_summary: Annualised kiln, grinding and process-equipment cost excluding explicitly listed fuel, electricity and capture service purchases. Carbon prices excluded.
input_basis_notes: Direct thermal fuels, electricity and capture service only. Limestone and minor raw material flows are omitted; process emissions are represented separately.
emissions_boundary_notes: Direct plant emissions only. Process emissions represent clinker calcination at a cement-equivalent basis using clinker-factor assumptions.
rollout_limit_notes: Indicative upper-bound envelope only. SCM availability, waste-fuel supply and CCS infrastructure constraints are only partly represented.
availability_conditions: Conventional and low-clinker methods are available from 2025; CCS ramps gradually from 2030 via the max-share envelope.
evidence_summary: Strongly anchored to Australian CIF decarbonisation pathway evidence, cross-checked with current Australian production and international IEA intensity benchmarks.
derivation_method: Australian industry pathway synthesis. Clinker ratio and clinker process-factor evidence are converted to per-tonne cement-equivalent process emissions, while fuel and electricity coefficients reflect CIF pathway and current industry-energy data.
review_notes: CCS is probably necessary for very low clinker-sector emissions, but the Australian cost and rollout evidence is much weaker than for clinker-factor and fuel-switch changes.
candidate_expansion_pathway: Split to clinker kiln, SCM supply, cement grinding and CCS modules, with explicit clinker-to-cement material balances.
times_or_vedalang_mapping_notes: Can expand to explicit clinker and cement processes in TIMES with separate kiln and grinding technologies plus capture/storage links.

cost_components_summary: Annualised kiln, grinding and process-equipment cost excluding explicitly listed fuel, electricity and capture service purchases. Carbon prices excluded.
input_basis_notes: Calibration-oriented incumbent kiln fuel mix. Coal, gas and biomass shares were re-tuned in the balance-ready pack to improve 2025 commodity closure while preserving the incumbent cement route.
emissions_boundary_notes: Direct plant emissions only. Process emissions represent clinker calcination at a cement-equivalent basis using clinker-factor assumptions.
rollout_limit_notes: Indicative upper-bound envelope only. SCM availability, waste-fuel supply and CCS infrastructure constraints are only partly represented.
availability_conditions: Conventional and low-clinker methods are available from 2025; CCS ramps gradually from 2030 via the max-share envelope.

derivation_method: Australian industry pathway synthesis. Clinker ratio and clinker process-factor evidence are converted to per-tonne cement-equivalent process emissions, while fuel and electricity coefficients reflect CIF pathway and current industry-energy data.
review_notes: Balance-ready incumbent cement method. Intended for national reduced-form calibration, not plant-level replication.

times_or_vedalang_mapping_notes: Can expand to explicit clinker and cement processes in TIMES with separate kiln and grinding technologies plus capture/storage links.
